column7: Typo – Please note that this indicates a Global Correction that should be implemented in THREE ways in the following sequence:  LB 1)  "+ .(" —> "+ (" LB 2)  "+ ." —> "+ "  LB 3)  ".(" —> "("   OR   ".(" —> ". (" LB Transformation Number 3, is the only one with possible "crossfire". In other words, it may need to be resolved in one of two ways, but it would be worthwhile to tackle the issue if it still remains, after the First correction has been made.
Typo – Please note that this is a Follow-Up correction to give a clearer parsing to the compound formation.

column6: Please let me know if you intend to list the "Masculine Form", separately, in addition to the adjective in the Hierarchy List Display. My opinion it that it is not necessary, although such has been already implemented in AP. I like to have a consistent approach throughout, so please advise as to your preference.
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:I intend to do this. Tracking at https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/175

column7: Typo – Here again is the issue of the abbreviation "a." in italics, which indicates "adjective". Of course the letter "n" as been erroneously used in this case. Therefore, a Very Safe Global Correction is being presented as follows: "a n." —> "a a."

Typo – Please note that the "Masculine Gender" is confirmed by MW, VCP and SKD.  ALSO, again we have a Global Correction as the sequence: "m a. "  with the abbreviation "a." in italics, will ALWAYS indicate a mistake !  However, it may resolve in one of TWO ways depending upon the Actual Gender, being either "Masculine or Neuter".  Therefore: LB 1)  In the case of Neuter Gender the correction will be: "m a." —> "m a"  –  (while removing the italics of the letter 'a'). LB 2)  In the case of Masculine Gender the correction will be: "m a." —> "ḥ a"  –  (while removing the italics of the letter 'a').
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:Corrected 25 Apr 2026. Rest cases are tracked at https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/176
